prompt: We're running a hands-on design session for a new regulatory risk-reporting data product — we need to nail the data model, how it's built, and how it's governed. Who should be in the room?

assertions.text: Sizes it as a design / working / maker-level session and states so
The data-architecture/modelling stream contributes exactly ONE voice (Eva Timmermans, the maker) — Lotte Peters is NOT also ranked
assertions.rationale: THE failure case: Lotte (Lead) + Eva (her report) both ranked stacks one stream.

assertions.text: Lotte Peters, Anika Sharma and Fatima El-Youssef are marked 'represented by' (not in the ranked list)
assertions.rationale: Whole architecture chain collapses to one voice; trim above and below.

assertions.text: A separate data-engineering voice appears (Sofia Costa or David Cohen, one of them) as a genuine remit fork
assertions.rationale: Build is a different remit from model.

assertions.text: A separate data-governance voice appears (Floor van der Berg) as a genuine remit fork
assertions.rationale: Govern is a different remit from model/build.

assertions.text: No two ranked rows share the same stream tag (Stream column present, one row per stream)
Output is a table with full name, role, stream, rationale, and 1-10 score; no fabricated/unprofiled people in the ranked list
